indicators.label: RBI Repo Rate
indicators.detail: RBI held rates steady at 6.25% in Feb 2026. Rate cut expected in Q2FY26 if CPI sustains below 4.5%. Positive for NBFCs and rate-sensitive sectors.

indicators.label: CPI Inflation
indicators.detail: Retail inflation at 4.31% in Jan 2026, approaching RBI's 4% target. Lower food prices driving decline. Positive for consumption themes and rate cut bets.

indicators.label: GDP Growth FY26
indicators.detail: India GDP growth projected at 6.8% for FY26 by RBI. Manufacturing and infrastructure sectors driving growth. IT sector headwinds persist due to US slowdown.

indicators.label: GST Collections
indicators.detail: GST collections at ₹1.96L Cr in Feb 2026 — highest ever. Strong consumption signal. Fiscal deficit tracking at 4.9% of GDP vs 5.1% target.

indicators.label: USD / INR
indicators.detail: INR weakened due to FII outflows and global risk-off. Good for IT exporters; bad for importers. RBI intervening to prevent sharp depreciation.

indicators.label: Crude Oil (Brent)
indicators.detail: Iran-Israel conflict pushing crude higher. Each $10/bbl rise adds ~₹1L Cr to India's import bill. Negative for OMCs, aviation, paints. Positive for ONGC, Oil India.

indicators.label: FII Flows (MTD)
indicators.detail: FIIs have been net sellers for 4 consecutive months. Selling concentrated in IT, banking. DII buying has partially offset. Watch for reversal post US Fed guidance.

indicators.label: 10Y G-Sec Yield
indicators.detail: G-Sec yields falling on RBI liquidity infusion and rate cut expectations. Positive for bond markets, reduces cost of capital for corporate India.

indicators.label: Iran-Israel Conflict
indicators.detail: Middle East tensions elevating. Strait of Hormuz risk — 20% of global oil transits here. India negotiating with Iran for safe passage of 20 tankers. Watch crude oil closely.

indicators.label: US Fed Rate
indicators.detail: Fed on hold — 2 cuts projected in 2026. Higher US rates mean FII outflows from EMs including India. Any dovish pivot will be strongly positive for Indian equities.

indicators.label: India 10Y Bond
indicators.detail: Bond yields softening as RBI injects liquidity. Positive for real estate, NBFCs and utility companies. Supports higher DCF valuations across sectors.

indicators.label: Nifty P/E
indicators.detail: Nifty 50 P/E at 21.2x vs long-term average of 20x — moderately valued. Midcap and smallcap still at premium. Selective stock-picking recommended over index investing.
